extraction.one_line_summary: BISSEGGER Stefan won the 18km time trial by 0.83 seconds over Cavagna, taking yellow after Matthews lost 23 seconds on the uphill finish climb.
tactical_patterns: Dennis set fastest intermediate split at 7.7km but faded on uphill finish, revealing pure power specialists vulnerable on gradient.
Roglic 1.22s down at intermediate but closed to 3rd overall suggests Jumbo prioritizing positioning over peaking this early.
McNulty 9s down in 4th signals UAE developing second GC threat beyond Pogacar for stage racing depth.

rider_signals.BISSEGGER Stefan: positive: Track background enables superior bike handling on technical corners, won despite only second WorldTour stage race of career
rider_signals.DENNIS Rowan: negative: Fastest intermediate at 47km/h but lost 37s by finish reveals climber weakness on gradient finishes despite TT pedigree
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: negative: Lost yellow by 23s despite new TT bike suggests sprinter can't defend GC once gradients appear
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: Jumbo downplaying form but 3rd place only 10s down suggests controlled effort rather than weakness
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Remy: positive: French TT champion 0.83s from win confirms top-tier short TT ability on punchy courses
rider_signals.McNULTY Brandon: positive: 4th at 9s after UAE Tour runner-up confirms emerging TT threat at age 23
